quote: Cutting losses quickly is one of the most important rules of stock trading. Even the best traders are wrong a lot of the time.
It's not whether you're right or wrong, but how much money you make when you're right and how much you lose when you're wrong.
Trade what you see, not what you think.
It takes courage to be a pig.
Risk management is the most important thing to be well understood. It's a way to ensure ongoing survival.
The whole secret to winning big in the stock market is not how much you make on your winners, but how much you don't lose on your losers.
I look for stocks making new highs, not new lows.
I am much more interested in 'when' than 'why'. I don't need to know why a stock should go up; all I need to know is whether it is likely to go up.
The big money is made by sitting and being patient, not by trading every day.

Never, ever invest in the present. It doesn't matter what a company's earnings are, what they have been or what they will be. You have to visualize the situation 18 months from now, and whatever that is, that's where the price will be, not where it is today.
Earnings don't move the overall market; it's the Federal Reserve Board... focus on the central banks, and focus on the movement of liquidity. It's liquidity that moves markets.
To have lasting success in the stock market, you must decide once and for all that it's more important to make money than to be right. Your ego must take a backseat.
The way to build superior long-term returns is through preservation of capital and home runs.
Buy what you know — look at the brands around you, look at the items you use, the clothes you wear.
What I needed to do was to focus on stocks that strictly fit the criteria.
